title: rosa × alba var. incarnata
intro: Rosa × alba var. incarnata, also known as the sweetbriar, is a fragrant and flowering plant species that belongs to the rose family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, coughs, and respiratory issues, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Rosa × alba var. incarnata include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and act as a natural antiseptic. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or syrup, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
